verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is an ER-resident ligand-gated Ca2+ channel with six transmembrane domains anchored to the endoplasmic reticulum membrane, not the plasma membrane. The large cytosolic IP3-binding domain and channel architecture are wholly intracellular. No documented ectodomain shedding, plasma-membrane cycling, TM-partner tethering, cell-state-induced surfacing, tissue-restricted surface display, or other contextual mechanism allows the protein body to reach the outer leaflet of the cell surface. Not a binder target.
